element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to interact with environment via animated hand for throwing, tapping, casting miracles
Creature learns from player treatment; personality and behavior evolve based on training and moral choices
Expanding villages by constructing buildings, increasing villagers, assigning tasks
Good vs evil advisor influence; player actions affect alignment which changes land, interface, music, and temple appearance

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early gameplay once Village Centre constructed
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Cast via gesture or menu; includes food, wood, healing, shields; requires worship power or sacrifice
Trigger significant story events and main progression objectives
Minor tasks offering rewards including Miracle Dispensers
Special buildings granting specific benefits; tied to eight tribal factions (Norse, Celtic, Japanese, etc.)
Sacrificing living beings at altar generates power; escalates moral alignment toward evil
Terrorizing villagers with fireballs and lightning storms to force belief; direct combat against opponent god
Temple destruction = game over; generates miracles via worship; transferring damage to followers in defense
Extended by population growth or village conquest; limits where miracles can be cast
Opposing god seeking world conquest; primary campaign objective is defeat

typical_tension_triggers: Moral alignment pressure from dual advisors
Loss of worshippers reducing miracle power
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical resource management with narrative event interruptions; tension builds through multi-layered threat system (enemy gods, creature management, villager welfare, moral alignment); relief comes from successful micro-interventions and population growth milestones

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Scrolls (gold and silver) mark available objectives; artefacts glow in owner color to signal control and discovery

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-paced introduction to god hand, basic miracle casting, simple village expansion; single-island focus with limited enemy opposition
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Multi-island management; introduction of moral alignment consequences; expanded creature AI demands; competing gods begin active opposition
difficulty_curve.late_game: Simultaneous multi-island control; sustained creature personality development; escalated military/miracle combat; approaching final confrontation with Nemesis
notable_spikes: Transition to multi-island campaigns requiring parallel resource management

notable_level_patterns.description: Each island represents a complete cycle: player enters with limited influence, gradually expands village populations, gains worshipper power, constructs wonders tied to tribal identity, confronts local rival gods, achieves full island dominance before advancing
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Norse island (introductory), later Celtic and Japanese islands with increased complexity

notable_level_patterns.description: Good and evil advisors continuously attempt to pull player toward opposing moral poles, with environmental and interface feedback reflecting accumulated choices; player can ignore both for neutral stance

Avatar creature serves as extension of player will; its learned behaviors (good vs evil actions, problem-solving style, aggression level) evolve based on training, creating personalized gameplay variant per playthrough
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All islands; emerges strongest in mid-to-late game

notable_level_patterns.description: Specific miracles available only via Village Centre construction or silver scroll rewards; creates soft progression gate where players must earn access to powerful interventions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All islands; particularly relevant in early island phases
